scenario: The policy has a 30-day waiting period and covers theft. The customer purchased the policy on January 1. Their laptop was stolen on January 15. Should the claim be approved?
The policy has a 30-day waiting period and covers theft. The customer purchased the policy on January 1. Their laptop was stolen on February 10. Should the claim be approved?
The policy covers accidental damage and theft. Claims must be reported within 30 days. A customer's phone was accidentally damaged and reported 45 days later. Should the claim be approved?
The policy covers accidental damage and theft. Claims must be reported within 30 days. A customer's phone was accidentally damaged and reported 10 days later. Should the claim be approved?
The policy covers fire damage. The policy expired on March 1. A fire occurred on February 25 and the claim was filed on March 5. Should the claim be approved?
The policy covers fire damage. The policy expired on March 1. A fire occurred on March 5 and the claim was filed on March 10. Should the claim be approved?
The policy covers collision damage but excludes commercial vehicle use. The insured vehicle was being used for commercial delivery when the collision occurred. Should the claim be approved?
The policy covers collision damage but excludes commercial vehicle use. The insured vehicle was being used for personal transportation when the collision occurred. Should the claim be approved?

The policy covers theft after a 30-day waiting period. The policy was purchased on January 1. A laptop was stolen on February 10. Claims must be reported within 15 days. The theft was reported on February 20. Should the claim be approved?
The policy covers storm damage with a $1000 deductible. A storm caused $5000 in damage. Should the claim be approved?
The policy covers theft but excludes theft caused by negligence. The customer left the vehicle unlocked and valuables were stolen. Should the claim be approved?
The policy covers theft but excludes theft caused by negligence. The vehicle was locked and valuables were stolen after a break-in. Should the claim be approved?
The policy covers water damage but excludes gradual leaks. Damage occurred due to a pipe slowly leaking over six months. Should the claim be approved?
The policy covers water damage but excludes gradual leaks. Damage occurred due to a pipe suddenly bursting. Should the claim be approved?

The policy covers fire damage but excludes commercial property use. A fire damaged a building being used for commercial storage. The policy was active and the claim was filed within the required reporting period. Should the claim be approved?
